content.full_text:
January 9,
1995
Sgt.
Paul R.
Curry
Legislative Liaison
San Bernardino County Sheriff’s Dept.
P.O.
Box 569
San Bernardino,
CA
92402-0569
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-385
Dear Sgt.
Curry:
This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
Yucaipa City Councilman Norwood Williams under the conflict-of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
1
!
QUESTION
As an employee with the San Bernardino County Sheriff’s
Department,
may Councilmember Williams participate in the
negotiations and decision to contract with the San Bernardino
County Sheriff’s Department to provide law enforcement services to
the City of Yucaipa?
CONCLUSION
Councilmember Williams’ status as an employee for the County
of San Bernardino does not create a disqualifying financial
interest in a decision of the Yucaipa City Council.
He is not
prohibited from participating in the negotiations and the ultimate
decision to contract with the County of San Bernardino for law
enforcement services.
FACTS
Councilmember Norwood Williams is a deputy sheriff in charge
of the Organizational Development Bureau of the San Bernardino
County Sheriff’s Department.
He has no direct supervision on a
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.


Advice File No.
A-94—385
Page
2
day—to—day basis over the commander or staff of the law
enforcement unit providing law enforcement to the City of Yucaipa.
Councilmember Williams plays no role in the negotiation of the
contract on behalf of the sheriff’s department with the City of
Yucaipa.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using his official position
to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a
financial interest.
An official has a financial interest in a
governmental decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public
generally,
on the official,
or on a member of the official’s im
mediate family,
or on:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(c)
Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d)
Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.
The definition of “income” in Section 82030 specifically
excludes salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem
received from a state,
local or federal government agency.
Thus,
the salary Councilmember Williams receives from the San Bernardino
County Sheriff’s Department does not create a conflict of interest
with respect to decisions affecting the City of Yucaipa or San
Bernardino County.
In addition,
a local government agency is not an organization
or enterprise operated for profit, it is, therefore,
not a
“business entity” as defined by the Act.
Councilmember Williams’
status as an employee of San Bernardino County will not create a
conflict of interest concerning decisions affecting the county.


Advice File No.
A-94-385
Page
3
Thus,
absent some other disqualifying financial interest as
set forth in Section 87103,
above,
or some direct financial effect
on Councilmember Williams personally resulting from a governmental
decision,
Councilmember Williams may participate in city council
decisions concerning San Bernardino County.
Should you have any further questions concerning this matter,
please feel free to contact the legal division at
(916)
322—5901.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division
SH/JETIjt

QUESTION: As an employee with the San Bernardino County Sheriff’s
Department,
may Councilmember Williams participate in the
negotiations and decision to contract with the San Bernardino
County Sheriff’s Department to provide law enforcement services to
the City of Yucaipa?

CONCLUSION: Councilmember Williams’ status as an employee for the County
of San Bernardino does not create a disqualifying financial
interest in a decision of the Yucaipa City Council.
He is not
prohibited from participating in the negotiations and the ultimate
decision to contract with the County of San Bernardino for law
enforcement services.
